effort_complexity.rationale: See spec.json; the file surface is bounded, but refresh scheduling and atomic navigation-safe snapshot replacement are state-machine work.

release_benefit: A SwornAgent operator can monitor in-flight release boards in an open TUI and see the newest completed shared-catalog snapshot without restarting or losing navigation context.

positive_scenarios: An open TUI gains a newly added slice in both the releases aggregate and selected board after one background catalog discovery completes.
Release and slice selections survive catalog insertion and reordering by stable identity.
negative_scenarios: A slow discovery cannot overlap another or let an older generation replace newer visible state.
A transient catalog failure cannot erase the last good board or permanently stop refresh.
Catalog refresh cannot interfere with the existing live-view or log-view tick chains.
validation.benefit_hypothesis: Automatic serial refresh turns the ref-aware TUI from a startup snapshot viewer into a trustworthy monitor for releases that continue changing while the process is open.

design_decisions.choice: Use a non-overlapping asynchronous refresh chain and atomically replace list and selected board from one catalog result.
options: startup snapshot plus restart or manual refresh
fixed ticks that may overlap catalog discovery
serial background discovery re-armed only after completion
design_decisions.human_decision: Repository owner required an open TUI to monitor in-flight releases on 2026-07-18; the serial automatic option directly satisfies that requirement.
design_decisions.rationale: Serial discovery bounds Git work and atomic replacement preserves the shared catalog as the single list-and-board authority.
